recipeService.title: Get a custom AI video shot plan + prompt pack.
recipeService.description: Use the free recipes now, or hire aiscreens to turn your product, course, app, or local business into ready-to-generate short-form concepts.

homepageOffer.title: Free learning now. Paid director workflows later.
homepageOffer.description: The site stays useful for beginners, while advanced creators and clients can pay for saved workflows, premium packs, model exports, and done-for-you prompt systems.

plans.name: Creator
plans.description: Saved projects, premium templates, more preview clips, and prompt variants.

plans.name: Pro / Service
plans.description: Shot plans, prompt packs, storyboard exports, and client-ready AI video concepts.

landingDemo.title: It does not just explain film terms. It directs the next clip.
landingDemo.description: A beginner arrives with a rough idea. aiscreens turns that idea into a visual path: what to shoot, what to prompt, what can fail, and what to try next.

steps.title: Describe the video
steps.label: Brief
steps.copy: New cafe launch reel for Instagram
steps.note: Start with the business goal, not random style words.

steps.title: Build the shot path
steps.label: Plan
steps.copy: Exterior -> barista action -> macro pour -> customer payoff
steps.note: The user sees how the clip should unfold before spending credits.

steps.title: Preview the motion
steps.label: Motion
steps.copy: Slow push-in, steam drift, handoff, clean ending
steps.note: A small moving storyboard explains what the prompt means.

steps.title: Paste into a model
steps.label: Export
steps.note: The output tells beginners where to paste and what to fix if it fails.

creditFixer.title: Catch weak prompts before they become expensive random clips.
creditFixer.description: For beginners, the hard part is knowing what is missing. This panel teaches the boring checks that save money: subject, action, camera, light, duration, and ending.
creditFixer.rewritePrompt: macro close-up of a matte black smartwatch on a stone table, soft side light, slow push-in, screen glow turns on, clean ending, 5 seconds

previewWall.title: Learn shots by seeing them move.
previewWall.description: Short preview clips make the glossary feel like a visual playground, not a dictionary.

homepageRecipes.title: Start with a recipe, not a film textbook.
homepageRecipes.description: Pick the kind of video you want to make. aiscreens gives you a shot plan, preview examples, and prompts to paste into your AI video tool.
homepageRecipes.ctaLabel: Make a plan from a recipe
homepageRecipes.gridTitle: What do you want to make?
homepageRecipes.gridDescription: Choose a video type and get a beginner-friendly director plan with copyable AI prompts.

painPoints.title: Random clips
painPoints.description: Replace vague prompts with shot order, subject, action, camera, light, and duration.

painPoints.title: Weak continuity
painPoints.description: Plan context, action, detail, emotion, and payoff before generating separate clips.

painPoints.title: Credit waste
painPoints.description: Check the shot idea and copy a tighter prompt before paying for another render.

liveExample.title: From messy idea to usable shot plan
liveExample.description: A beginner does not need to know film language first. They need to see how one vague idea becomes shots they can actually generate.
liveExample.prompt: cinematic close-up of hot coffee being poured into a ceramic cup, soft morning light, cozy cafe background, shallow depth of field, 5 seconds

workflowSteps.title: Three steps to AI-ready shots
workflowSteps.description: From zero to copy-ready prompts in minutes.

steps.title: Pick a recipe
steps.description: Start with a real creator job: product reel, coffee shop video, travel montage, tutorial, or interview.

steps.title: Generate a director plan
steps.description: Enter your topic and get a prioritized shot order with what each shot does and why it belongs there.

steps.title: Copy model-ready prompts
steps.description: Choose Generic, Runway, Pika, Sora, or Kling/Veo export and paste the prompt where you generate.

beginnerPath.title: Start here. Learn the basics in order.
beginnerPath.description: You do not need to know film words. This path teaches the exact ideas a beginner needs before writing AI video prompts.

items.label: What is a shot?
items.detail: Understand the smallest unit of a video.

items.label: Wide, Medium, Close-Up
items.detail: Learn how shot size changes what viewers feel.

items.label: Camera angles
items.detail: Use height and perspective with purpose.

items.label: Camera movement
items.detail: Add motion only when it helps the idea.

items.label: Basic lighting
items.detail: Make the subject readable before making it stylish.

items.label: Your first AI prompt
items.detail: Combine subject, action, camera, light, and duration.

items.title: Learn the Language
items.description: Short visual definitions for shots, lighting, camera movement, and AI prompting terms.

items.title: Plan the Sequence
items.description: Turn a rough idea into a shot order with purpose, failure notes, and model-ready prompts.

items.title: Practice the Basics
items.description: Build local progress by learning the smallest concepts first, then layering real video grammar.

items.title: Use a Recipe
items.description: Start from real creator jobs like product reels, cafe videos, interviews, tutorials, and tours.

servicePage.title: Bring one idea. Leave with a usable AI video plan.
servicePage.description: For creators, ecommerce brands, course builders, local businesses, and small agencies that want better AI video outputs without wasting credits on trial and error.

packages.name: Prompt Pack

deliverables: Model-ready prompts for Runway, Pika, Sora, Kling, or Veo
Common failure checklist and retry notes

packages.name: Content System
packages.bestFor: Brands that need multiple short-form concepts or a repeatable content engine.
deliverables: Storyboard-style shot order for each concept
Prompt variants for product, UGC, tutorial, and ad styles

packages.name: Workflow Build
packages.bestFor: Teams with a boring repeated video problem that needs a pipeline.

process.title: Send the goal
process.description: Share the product, lesson, service, brand notes, references, and the AI video model you want to use.

process.title: We plan the video
process.description: aiscreens converts the idea into shots, prompts, continuity notes, and retry guidance before you generate.

process.title: You generate with less waste
process.description: Use the pack inside your model, then refine with specific failure fixes instead of starting over.

briefBuilder.title: Write the first client email in 60 seconds.
briefBuilder.description: No backend required yet. A small guided brief makes the paid path feel real and helps clients send the details needed for a useful prompt pack.

fields.name: project
fields.label: What are we making?
fields.placeholder: Example: 20 second product reel for a handmade candle

fields.name: audience
fields.label: Who should watch it?
fields.placeholder: Example: Instagram buyers who like cozy home decor

fields.name: model
fields.label: Preferred AI video model
fields.placeholder: Runway, Pika, Sora, Kling, Veo, or not sure yet

fields.name: references
fields.label: References or constraints
fields.placeholder: Brand colors, product images, mood, must-avoid details, deadline

fitChecklist: You have a product, lesson, service, app, or brand story to explain.
You want AI video outputs with fewer random generations.
You need shot order, prompts, and retry notes more than a generic inspiration board.
You are using or planning to use Runway, Pika, Sora, Kling, Veo, or another AI video tool.

faqs.question: Do you generate the final video?
faqs.answer: The first service offer is planning and prompt direction. If final generation is needed, the workflow can be scoped manually after the shot plan is clear.

faqs.question: What do I need to send?
faqs.answer: Send the goal, audience, product or topic details, references if available, preferred AI video model, and any brand constraints.

faqs.question: Why pay if the planner is free?
faqs.answer: The free planner gives a strong starting point. The paid service adds research, sequencing judgment, model-specific notes, and a client-ready prompt pack.

onboardingFlow.title: You have an idea, but the AI video result is random.
onboardingFlow.description: Most beginners start with style words and waste credits because the subject, motion, camera, light, and ending are not planned.

onboardingFlow.title: aiscreens teaches the visual language as you need it.
onboardingFlow.description: Glossary cards stay compact first, then expand into prompts, mistakes, related terms, and practical usage only when the user asks.

onboardingFlow.title: The planner turns one rough topic into a shot sequence.
onboardingFlow.description: Instead of one overloaded prompt, users get a step-by-step director plan with shot purpose and model-specific export guidance.

onboardingFlow.title: Recipes give users a fast starting point.
onboardingFlow.description: Creator-friendly packs help people make product reels, tutorials, interviews, travel clips, and local business videos faster.

onboardingFlow.title: When users need it done for them, aiscreens becomes a service.
onboardingFlow.description: The free product builds trust. Paid work can start with custom shot plans, prompt packs, storyboard exports, and AI video concepts.

workflowDemo.title: Idea → shot plan → prompt → retry fix.
workflowDemo.description: aiscreens should feel like a tiny AI video director: it gives beginners a path before they open a generation model.

steps.title: Idea
steps.body: A local bakery wants a 12-second launch reel.
steps.caption: Start from the user’s real goal, not a random cinematic adjective stack.

steps.title: Shot Plan
steps.body: Wide shop exterior, product close-up, human handoff, final hero plate.
steps.caption: Break the clip into moments so the model has less to invent.

steps.title: Model Prompt
steps.body: Warm bakery interior, macro pastry detail, slow push-in, soft window light, 5 seconds.
steps.caption: Turn each shot into concrete subject, action, camera, light, and time.

steps.title: Retry Fix
steps.body: If the pastry changes shape, lock product description and reduce camera motion.
steps.caption: Fix the failure type instead of paying for another vague retry.

modelTips.copy: Use one clear camera move, concrete subject, lighting, and temporal direction.
Keep the action small, describe the subject plainly, and avoid stacking many movements.
Write the scene like a short director note: subject, setting, action, camera, and ending.
Use cinematic language with a precise payoff shot so the clip does not drift.

audiencePaths.label: Course creator
audiencePaths.problem: Needs lesson hooks, b-roll ideas, and clean explainer sequences without learning film school first.
audiencePaths.workflow: Turn one lesson topic into a 5-shot teaching plan with intro, visual proof, screen detail, recap, and CTA.

audiencePaths.label: Ecommerce brand
audiencePaths.problem: Needs product videos that do not morph, lose detail, or feel like random cinematic stock footage.
audiencePaths.workflow: Lock product identity, choose close-up/detail/payoff shots, then export prompts with failure checks.

audiencePaths.label: Solo creator
audiencePaths.problem: Needs more ideas and better prompts without burning credits on vague experiments.
audiencePaths.workflow: Start from a recipe, learn the shot terms only when needed, and copy model-ready prompts.

audiencePaths.label: Local business
audiencePaths.problem: Needs simple videos for cafes, salons, gyms, clinics, events, and services without hiring a full crew.
audiencePaths.workflow: Use practical shot order: location, person, detail, transformation, and final offer.
